questions.q: The Pashupatastra was the personal weapon of which god?
options: Vishnu
Brahma
Shiva
Indra
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra belonged to Shiva, who granted it to no one lightly.

questions.q: Which mortal hero received the Pashupatastra in the Mahabharata?
options: Arjuna
Karna
Bhishma
Ashwatthama
questions.explanation: Shiva granted the weapon to Arjuna after testing him in the form of a mountain hunter.

questions.q: How did Arjuna earn the Pashupatastra?
options: By winning a debate with the mountain sages
Through fierce penance and combat with a disguised Shiva
By offering Shiva the bow Gandiva
Through a horse sacrifice in the Himalayas
questions.explanation: Arjuna performed severe austerities and then fought the god himself, who had come disguised as a kirata hunter.

questions.q: What was the Pashupatastra said to be capable of?
options: Burning one army to ash each day
Slaying any single chosen enemy
Splitting the earth down to its roots
Destroying the whole of creation
questions.explanation: The weapon was held able to unmake creation itself, which is why its use was so tightly bound.

questions.q: How many times did Arjuna loose the Pashupatastra during the eighteen days of Kurukshetra?
options: Not even once
Once, on the final day
Twice, against Karna and Ashwatthama
Thrice, at Krishna's signal
questions.explanation: Arjuna carried the deadliest weapon of the war through all eighteen days and never released it.

questions.q: Where did Arjuna win Shiva's great weapon?
options: At Kurukshetra's edge
On Mount Indrakila in the Himalayas
In Indra's city of Amaravati
On the banks of the Saraswati
questions.explanation: The penance, the duel, and the bestowal all took place on Mount Indrakila during the forest exile.

questions.q: From which name of Shiva does the weapon take its title?
options: Pashupati, lord of creatures
Nataraja, lord of the dance
Bhairava, the terrifier
Nilakantha, the blue-throated
questions.explanation: Pashupatastra means the weapon of Pashupati, Shiva as the lord of all bound creatures.

questions.q: Against whom was Arjuna warned never to hurl the Pashupatastra?
options: Warriors older than himself
Anyone of his own blood
Lesser foes unworthy of it
Enemies already in retreat
questions.explanation: Shiva forbade its use against lesser or undeserving opponents, reserving it for the mightiest of foes.

questions.q: What danger came with misusing the weapon against a feeble target?
options: It would strike the wielder's family
It might consume the whole universe
It would return to Shiva forever
It would split into a thousand curses
questions.explanation: Hurled at an unworthy mark, the weapon's fury could swallow the entire world, not just the target.

questions.q: During which chapter of the Pandavas' lives did Arjuna acquire the Pashupatastra?
options: Their training under Drona
Their rule from Indraprastha
Their twelve-year forest exile
Their year hidden in Virata's court
questions.explanation: The quest for divine weapons, crowned by Shiva's gift, belongs to the forest years of the exile.

questions.q: By which means could the Pashupatastra be discharged?
options: Only from the Gandiva at high noon
Only with Shiva's name spoken thrice
Only after a full day of fasting and prayer
By thought, by the eye, by word, or by bow
questions.explanation: The epic says the weapon could be launched by the mind, the glance, speech, or the bow, a power no other astra shared.

questions.q: What had Arjuna just done when Shiva chose to grant him the weapon?
options: Wrestled the disguised god with his bare hands
Recited the hundred names of Rudra
Slain the demon Muka's whole retinue
Stood a full year on one foot
questions.explanation: It was Arjuna's fearless hand-to-hand fight against the disguised god that won Shiva's delight and the weapon.

questions.q: What twin knowledge came with the weapon's bestowal?
options: Its forging and its breaking
Its launching and its recall
Its naming and its hiding
Its blessing and its curse
questions.explanation: Shiva taught Arjuna both how to release the astra and how to withdraw it, knowledge as vital as the weapon itself.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata is the granting of the Pashupatastra told?
options: Adi Parva
Bhishma Parva
Vana Parva
Shanti Parva
questions.explanation: The bestowal follows the Kirata duel in the Vana Parva, the book of the forest exile.

questions.q: How exclusive was the Pashupatastra said to be?
options: Each lokapala kept one copy
All eleven rudras bore it in war
Brahma issued it once each age
Even Indra, Yama, and Varuna did not possess it
questions.explanation: Shiva declared that the weapon was unknown even to the great gods such as Indra, Yama, Kubera, and Varuna.

questions.q: What, according to Shiva, could withstand the Pashupatastra?
options: Nothing in the three worlds
Only Mount Meru's roots
Only the primeval ocean
Only Vishnu's own discus
questions.explanation: Shiva told Arjuna that nothing moving or unmoving in the three worlds could stand against the weapon.

questions.q: What deeper meaning is often read into Arjuna carrying the weapon through the whole war without using it?
options: That Shiva's gifts fade when untested
That the weapon was merely ceremonial
The discipline of holding ultimate power in restraint
That Arjuna doubted the god who gave it
questions.explanation: The unused Pashupatastra is commonly read as a lesson in restraint, supreme power mastered rather than spent.

questions.q: For what purpose had Arjuna sought weapons of the gods in the first place?
options: To conquer the kingdoms of the north
To face champions like Bhishma, Drona, and Karna in war
To guard the Kamyaka hermitages from rakshasas
To win back Indraprastha without battle
questions.explanation: The Pandavas knew war was coming, and only celestial weapons could answer the great Kaurava champions.

questions.q: What omens are described at the moment the weapon came to Arjuna?
options: Rivers paused and birds fell silent
A ring circled the noonday sun
Flowers rained on the Kaurava court
The earth trembled and unseen conches and drums sounded
questions.explanation: When the astra took its place with Arjuna, the earth shook to its mountains and invisible conches and drums resounded.

questions.q: Which demon, in beast form, attacked Arjuna just before his battle with the disguised Shiva?
options: Muka
Baka
Kirmira
Alambusha
questions.explanation: The rakshasa Muka charged Arjuna as a boar, and the two simultaneous arrows that felled him began the quarrel.

questions.q: What did Shiva's touch do to the battered Arjuna before the weapon was given?
options: Set a third eye in his brow
Healed his wounds and filled him with greater might
Turned his skin to adamant for a year
Burned away his memory of the fight
questions.explanation: Shiva's touch made Arjuna whole again and left him with greater radiance and strength than before.

questions.q: What did Arjuna do the moment he recognized the hunter as Shiva?
options: He offered the god his two quivers
He strung the Gandiva in salute
He vowed to build a shrine on Indrakila
He fell at the god's feet, begging forgiveness for the fight
questions.explanation: Arjuna prostrated himself and asked pardon for having battled the god, and Shiva, delighted, forgave him.

questions.q: Before slaying Jayadratha, how did Arjuna renew his command of Shiva's weapon?
options: By a night vigil at a wayside linga
By fasting until the moon rose
In a dream-journey with Krishna to Shiva's abode
By bathing in the Saraswati at dawn
questions.explanation: In the Drona Parva, Arjuna travels with Krishna in a dream to Shiva and receives the Pashupata lore afresh before the fourteenth day.

questions.q: Late in life, escorting the Yadava women after Krishna's death, what did Arjuna discover about his celestial weapons?
options: They would no longer answer his mind
Only the Pashupatastra still obeyed him
They worked, but Gandiva had warped
They had passed to Parikshit unasked
questions.explanation: In the Mausala Parva his astras failed to come to mind against the Abhira robbers, for their hour had passed with Krishna.

questions.q: How is the Pashupatastra described as attending Arjuna after the gift?
options: As a flame hidden within the Gandiva
As a black arrow in his quiver
As a mantra etched upon his palm
Standing beside him in embodied form
questions.explanation: The epic says the weapon waited upon Arjuna in an embodied form once it had been bestowed.

questions.q: Who was with Arjuna when he received the Pashupatastra?
options: Krishna, watching unseen
No one, for he had gone into the mountains alone
Bhima, waiting below the peak
Dhaumya, chanting at his side
questions.explanation: Arjuna had left his brothers behind and undertaken the penance and the duel entirely alone.

questions.q: What did Arjuna behold when the hunter finally revealed himself?
options: Shiva in glory with Parvati at his side
A pillar of fire splitting the mountain
A thousand-headed serpent of light
The entire war that was yet to come
questions.explanation: The kirata stood transformed into Shiva in his divine splendour, with Parvati beside him.

questions.q: How is the Pashupatastra commonly ranked among the weapons present at Kurukshetra?
options: A lesser astra beside the Narayanastra
Equal to Indra's Vasavi Shakti
Second to the Brahmastra in terror
The deadliest of all, though never loosed
questions.explanation: It is remembered as the most terrible weapon carried onto that field, made remarkable by never being used.

questions.q: Who brought the waiting Pandavas word that Arjuna had won Shiva's weapon and now sat in Indra's heaven?
options: Narada, descending mid-song
A gandharva of Chitrasena's court
The sage Lomasha, sent from Indra's hall
Dhaumya, through a vision at dawn
questions.explanation: Indra sent the sage Lomasha down with the news, and Lomasha then guided the brothers on pilgrimage.

questions.q: By which month of his penance was Arjuna living on air alone?
options: The second
The fourth
The sixth
The ninth
questions.explanation: He ate fruit every third night, then every sixth, then each fortnight, until in the fourth month he lived on air.
